commit.message:
Editorial review: terminology consistency across all 6 weeks

- Replace all "patron/client" language with "member" throughout weeks 1–6
- Fix "Patron (best term)" → "Member (best term)" in terminology table,
  quick reference guide, and course scenarios
- Correct IRS 14-point checklist: Week 1 covers 5 points (not 4); add
  Point 6 checklist items; fix exercise title to reflect actual coverage
- Fix banking section in Week 3: both direct church account (Option A)
  and LLC account (Option B) are valid; LLC not mandatory
- Reorder Week 3 module 3.3: "Defining Your Auxiliary Identity" now
  precedes "Understanding Your Letter of Direction"
- Remove LGBTQ+ demographic targeting from Week 3 module 3.3
- Replace sage/smudging/sweat lodge references with tradition-agnostic
  language or add cultural protocol notes (weeks 3–5)
- Add Formation Toolkit and service template download sections (weeks 3, 4)
- Add three complete service templates: individual healing session,
  group healing circle, new moon ceremony
- Add auxiliary charter and IRS compliance checklist templates

commit.message:
Replace DOM measurement with pre-computed handle positions

The dynamic handle positioning used useLayoutEffect + getBoundingClientRect
in a two-render cycle that caused timing/coordinate misalignment with React
Flow's handle system (handles floating below node, appearing on wrong fields).

Now computes handle positions arithmetically from column group data using
CSS pixel constants, eliminating the measurement cycle entirely.

fix: move $3k website-only note above package feature list

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: replace local MCP child processes with Bright Data remote MCP

Eliminates npx @brightdata/mcp spawning (150MB per process) in favour
of direct HTTP calls to mcp.brightdata.com. All scrapers now run fully
in parallel with zero process overhead — no semaphore, no queue, no
startup latency. Removes @brightdata/mcp dependency and the
--max-old-space-size workaround.

fix: eliminate re-render loops on wake from sleep (#100)

* fix: eliminate all visual jank sources in GTM Kanban board

- Remove transition: 'all .15s' from task cards (caused unexpected
  visual effects on any CSS property change)
- Replace transition: 'all .2s' with targeted transitions on filter
  buttons and user view tabs
- Replace direct DOM style mutations in drag handlers with React
  state (dragOverCol) for proper reconciliation
- Clean up onDragEnd to reset both dragId and dragOverCol
- Add useCallback import for future optimization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove autosave debounce, drag-and-drop, and card hover jank from GTM board

- Replace scheduleSave (600ms debounce timer) with immediate saveBoard
- Remove all drag-and-drop (dragId, dragOverCol, onDragStart, onDragEnd, onDragOver, onDragLeave, onDrop, draggable)
- Remove className="card" from MRR progress bar to eliminate CSS hover transitions
- Tasks now moved only via arrow buttons, saves happen instantly on action

Add minWidth: 0 and overflow: hidden to column containers so CSS grid
columns respect 1fr width instead of expanding to fit content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: allow super admins to invite team members

- Frontend: treat app_role='admin' as team owner in TeamsPage so they
  can see the Settings tab and use the invite UI
- Backend: invite-member edge function now checks caller's app_role
  and allows admins to bypass the team.created_by ownership check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: check team owner's plan (not caller's) when admin sends invite

The plan/billing check used userId (the caller) to look up the plan,
but when a super admin sends the invite, their profile has no paid plan.
Now uses team.created_by to check the actual team owner's plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error message in invite-member for debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error on invite failure instead of generic message

Catch network/CORS errors separately, handle non-JSON responses,
and show HTTP status code when error body is empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: disable gateway JWT verification for invite-member edge function

The Supabase gateway was rejecting requests with 401 before our
function code ran, returning non-JSON without CORS headers. Our
function already handles JWT verification internally (lines 40-57),
so gateway verification is redundant and prevents proper error responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: super admins bypass plan/billing and member limit checks for invites

Super admins (app_role='admin') now skip all plan, billing, and member
count checks when sending invites. Previously failed because admin
plan is not in PLAN_MAX_MEMBERS map.

commit.message:
fix: URL-encode CDN path to prevent urllib crash on spaces

Nexus Mods CDN URLs can contain unescaped spaces in filenames
(e.g. "Legendary Endings-..."). Since Python 3.7.2, urllib rejects
these as "URL can't contain control characters". Fix by parsing and
re-encoding the URL path before the request.

Fix: exempt /api/scan from cookie auth middleware

The scan endpoint has its own bearer token auth, so the
middleware cookie check was blocking it with a 401.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: thread ?next= param through entire auth flow

User now continues to their original destination after login or
password reset instead of being dumped on a hardcoded page.

- Middleware: passes ?next=/deal-updater (or /admin) when redirecting
  unauthenticated users to /login
- Login page: reads ?next=, uses it after sign-in, threads it through
  the password reset email redirectTo chain
- Confirm page: reads ?next=, redirects there after password set
- Also fixes login sign-in to use window.location.href (same
  bulletproof pattern as sign-out) instead of router.push

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: read CORS_ORIGINS env var for production domain allowlisting

The CORS middleware only allowed localhost origins. Production
domains from the CORS_ORIGINS env var were never read, causing
"Failed to fetch" errors when the web app called the API.

commit.message:
fix(wasm): coerce string parameters to schema-declared types (#498)

* fix(wasm): coerce string parameters to schema-declared types

LLMs frequently pass numeric values as JSON strings ("5" instead of 5)
or booleans as strings ("true" instead of true). The WASM module's
serde deserializer rejects these type mismatches. This adds a
coerce_params_to_schema() helper that walks the params JSON object
and converts string values to their schema-declared types (number,
integer, boolean) before passing to the WASM module.

Adds 5 unit tests covering number, integer, boolean coercion,
already-correct types, and unparseable strings.

Closes #486

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: use in-place mutation and case-insensitive boolean coercion

Address review feedback:
- Use get_mut instead of clone+insert to avoid allocations
- Make boolean coercion case-insensitive (handles "True", "FALSE", etc.)
- Expand boolean test to cover false and mixed-case values

Fix: replace green accents with gold and fix map proxy path forwarding

- Swap all rgba(39,255,107,…) green accents to rgba(212,175,55,…) gold in .badge, .ip-address CSS, and copyIP() JS flash
- Rename --green CSS variable to --gold (#d4af37)
- Refactor proxy() to accept { url, origin } object mode so pre-built upstream URLs are used as-is (path not overwritten)
- Update /maps/system and /maps/world routes to use object mode, preserving the stripped pathname; leave SQM fallback call unchanged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: tournament engine — break status, chip_transaction fields, atomic prize credit

1. TournamentTimerService: Removed invalid 'paused' status write during breaks.
   The CHECK constraint only allows REGISTERING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED,
   ANNOUNCED, LATE_REG — 'paused' would fail silently. Breaks now just pause
   the local timer while tournament stays RUNNING in DB.

2. TournamentEngine: Fixed chip_transactions inserts using wrong field name
   'transaction_type' → 'type' (matching actual DB column). Also fixed
   'cashout' → 'cash_out' to match TransactionType enum.

3. TournamentEngine.creditPrize(): Upgraded to RPC-first atomic pattern
   (fn_add_chips) with read-modify-write fallback, matching the same fix
   applied to TableService.leaveTable() in the previous commit.

commit.message:
fix: correct local_path to use storage/app/private/media (Laravel 11 local disk)

The local disk root is storage/app/private/, not storage/app/. Files are at
storage/app/private/media/ but local_path pointed to storage/app/media/,
causing multipart uploads to fall back to URL method (which Telegram can't reach).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enable proper fork recursion with dynamic parent resolution and depth tracking

Three structural changes that make recursive forks work correctly:

1. Dynamic parent resolution: runFork now uses the caller agent (via
   callerAgentName from ToolCall) instead of the hardcoded parentAgentName.
   A sub-fork inherits its parent fork's context, not the original root
   agent's. Falls back to parentAgentName for backward compat.

2. Per-agent depth tracking: agentDepths map tracks each ephemeral agent's
   fork depth. Depth checks and tool filtering now use the caller's actual
   depth instead of the module's static currentDepth (which was always 0).
   At maxDepth, subagent:* tools are stripped so the deepest agents can't
   fork further.

3. Concurrency raised from 3 to 5: with zombie recovery in place, higher
   concurrency is safe and prevents recursive forks from starving siblings.
